welcome.title: Welcome to the API Hub!
welcome.desc: Let's show you around. This quick tour takes less than a minute.

sidebar.title: Sidebar
sidebar.desc: This is where you'll find Chat, API Keys, Billing, and your account settings.

modelPicker.title: Model Picker
modelPicker.desc: Choose which AI model to talk to here. We support 300+ models from 12+ providers.

userMenu.title: Your Account
userMenu.desc: Your API keys and account settings live here. Manage keys, view usage, and update your profile.

done.title: You're all set!
done.desc: You can replay this tour any time from the help menu.

quotas.a: Quotas limit how many credits or tokens you can consume over different windows: 5-hour, weekly, and monthly. Each window resets independently. Your admin can also set a hard budget cap. When a window resets, your usage counter goes back to zero but your budget cap does not.

quotaHit.q: What happens when I hit my quota?
quotaHit.a: Once you reach the limit for a given window, new API requests will be rejected with a 429 (rate limit) or 402 (payment required) status code. You'll need to wait for the window to reset, or ask your admin to increase your quota. You can check your current usage on the Usage page.

billing.a: Each model has a per-1k-token price for input and output tokens. When you make an API call, we count the input tokens (your prompt) and output tokens (the model's reply) and multiply by the respective price. The total is deducted from your wallet balance in credits. Some plans offer discounts on specific models.

errorCodes.a: 401 — Your API key or session is invalid or expired. Re-login or rotate your key. 402 — Insufficient balance or quota exceeded. Top up your wallet or wait for quota reset. 403 — You don't have permission to access this resource (e.g., model not in your allowed list). 429 — Rate limit exceeded. Too many requests in a short period; wait and retry. 500 — Internal server error on our side. Please retry or contact support.

channels.a: Channels are the upstream connections to AI providers. Each channel has a provider, a weight (for load balancing), and a cooldown period. When a request comes in, the system picks a healthy channel based on weight. If a channel fails, it enters cooldown and traffic shifts to other channels automatically (failover).

rotateKey.q: How do I rotate my API key?
rotateKey.a: Go to your Account page, find the API key you want to rotate, and click the Rotate button. A new key will be generated and the old one will be immediately invalidated. Make sure to update all your integrations with the new key before rotating.

usageHistory.q: Where can I see my usage history?
usageHistory.a: Navigate to the Usage page from the sidebar. You can filter by time range (today, last 7 days, last 30 days, this month, or custom), and break down usage by model, provider, or day. You can also export your usage data as CSV.

contactSupport.a: If you can't find an answer here, reach out to your administrator directly. If you are the administrator, check the backend logs at /admin/logs for detailed error information, or consult the project documentation.
